summary: The main claim is: However, there are a number of goals and values in the human environment that are not well-correlated with inclusive fitness.

For superintelligent AGI to be aligned with human values, therefore, it is much more necessary for its goals to be directly aligned with human values, even more than the degree to which human values are aligned with inclusive evolutionary…

top_comment.author: Vladimir_Nesov
top_comment.text:
Instrumental convergence makes differences in values hard to notice, so there can be abudant examples of misalignment that remain unobtrusive. The differences only become a glaring problem with enough inequality of power when coercing or outright overwriting others becomes feasible (Fnargl only reaches the coercing stage, but not overwriting stage). Thus even differences in values between humans and randomly orthogonal AGIs can seem non-threatening until they aren't, the same as differences in human values can remain irrelevant for average urban dwellers.

Alignment on values becomes crucial given overwriting-level power differences, since a thought experiment with putting a human in that position predicts a decent chance of non-doom, even though it's not a good plan on its own. Conversely, unfettered superintelligent paperclip maximizers or Fnargls don't leave survivors. And since the world is very far from an equilibrium of abundant superintelligence, there are going to be unprecedented power differentials while it's settling. This makes the common sense impression of irrelevance of misalignment on values (being masked by instrumental convergence) misleading when it comes to AGI.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: Instrumental convergence makes differences in values hard to notice, so there can be abudant examples of misalignment that remain unobtrusive.

If the button is not pressed, then the agent simply obtains its reward according to UN.

People with different loss functions get along well because each is willing to give something the other wants.

Information security is a support function needed by all organisations to different degrees.

top_comment.author: Max H
top_comment.text:
"Sundar is announcing" seems like a carefully chosen phrasing.

[...]

Yikes.

[...]

Maybe I'm reading too much into it, but the subtext I get from this letter is that this was very much not DeepMind or Demis's choice. There is no language indicating that he is pleased or happy with this decision, or that he believes it is the correct decision.  Just "thrilled" and "excited". This reads like it was written by someone who was forced to fall in line.

 Seems like bad news from the buying time / avoiding a race perspective.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: "Sundar is announcing" seems like a carefully chosen phrasing.

summary: The main claim is: I think we should have a similar set of regulatory oversight for artificial intelligence, because I think it is actually a bigger risk to society.” AI can be racist, sexist and creepy.

“Regulation “may slow down AI a little bit, but I think that that might also be a good thing,” Musk added.” Elon Musk warns AI ‘one of biggest risks’ to civilization during ChatGPT’s risk (NY Post) “I think we need to regulate AI safety, f…

top_comment.author: otto.barten
top_comment.text:
As co-author of one of the mentioned pieces, I'd say it's really great to see the AGI xrisk message mainstreaming. It doesn't nearly go fast enough, though. Some (Hawking, Bostrom, Musk) have already spoken out about the topic for close to a decade. So far, that hasn't been enough to change common understanding. Those, such as myself, who hope that some form of coordination could save us, should give all they have to make this go faster. Additionally, those who think regulation could work should work on robust regulation proposals which are currently lacking. And those who can should work on international coordination, which is currently also lacking.

A lot of work to be done. But the good news is that the window of opportunity is opening, and a lot of people could work on this which currently aren't. This could be a path to victory.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: And those who can should work on international coordination, which is currently also lacking.

summary: The main claim is: in this post i give an overview of the state of my AI alignment research, as well as what i think needs to be worked on, notably for people who might want to join my efforts.
The main claim is: Meanwhile, top AGI labs are following the Unified Evals Agreement.
The main claim is: DeepNash learns to play Stratego from scratch by combining game theory and model-free deep RL
The main claim is: Indeed, we were able to find adversarial examples based on our understanding of the IOI circuit.
How you can do this work We’re far from understanding everything about IOI, and there are many more exciting interpretability tasks to do.

The main claim is: General Altruistic Agents We should be able to achieve superhuman alignment using loose biological inspiration just as deep learning is progressing to superhuman capability using the same loose inspiration.
World design is what matters The central purpose of a simbox is to test and evaluate agent architectures for both capability and alignment .
